Monday, July 13, 2026 Daily Digest

AI Chatbots Own Discovery, Microsoft's Teams AI U-Turn, Google Hammers Thin Content

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

AI Chatbots Become the Front Door for Discovery

Similarweb data across thousands of shopping journeys reveals a clean split: people now lean on AI chatbots at the start of their research — when they're figuring out what they want — then shift to traditional search for price comparison. The kicker: visitors who arrive from chatbot recommendations view nearly 2x more pages and stay twice as long. If you're not showing up in those early AI conversations, you're not making the shortlist.

Read on Social Media Today →
2

Microsoft Makes Major AI U-Turn on Teams

Following user backlash over aggressive AI feature rollouts — particularly the "Facilitator" meeting monitor — Microsoft is letting Teams organizers turn AI features on or off during live meetings. The typical pattern: tech company floods users with AI features, users revolt, company walks it back. The lesson for enterprise AI: humans should decide where and when to use AI, not have it forced on them.

Read on TechRadar →
3

Google's June Spam Update Hit Fast and Hard

Google's June 2026 spam update rolled out in about two days and didn't feel gentle. Thin, spun, or heavily automated content is now a liability, not a shortcut. The silver lining: Google Search Console now shows AI performance data, so you can finally see how your site appears in AI-generated answers. LLMs.txt files won't help or hurt rankings — quality content is what wins.

Read on Search Engine Roundtable →
4

TikTok Bans AI Voices in Shopping Livestreams

TikTok now requires real humans for live commerce — banning AI-generated voices, recorded audio, and radio-style narration from shopping livestreams. Sellers were looping robotic AI narration all day, and viewers checked out. With TikTok Shop projected at $23.4B in US sales this year, the trust stakes are real: consumers are roughly 4x more likely to trust a brand less when they spot AI-generated content.

Read on Social Media Today →
5

SMBs "Vibe-Code" Their Way Off Salesforce

A growing trend: small businesses are using AI coding tools to build custom CRMs instead of paying for Salesforce. According to The Information, companies are cutting software costs by 40-80% after replacing Salesforce with vibe-coded applications built on Claude Code. Nearly 59% of Salesforce admins say the CRM is "becoming increasingly complex to work with." For enterprise SaaS, the disruption isn't just coming from AI-native startups — it's coming from customers who can now build their own.

Read on Salesforce Ben →

💡 My Take

The discovery shift is the story. The Similarweb data on AI chatbots owning the consideration stage is the biggest news today. If buyers are building their shortlists in conversations with ChatGPT and Perplexity before they ever hit Google, traditional SEO becomes table stakes while GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) becomes the differentiator. The question isn't whether to optimize for AI discovery — it's how fast you can get there.
